blocks.text: `Q-DIST-001` is resolved here: Claude, Codex, OpenClaw, and Cursor CLIs are external user-installed executables for the Mac App Store target. Riido may help users choose and verify paths, but provider execution remains behind the local helper and C4 runtime boundary.

items: `mac-app-store` must not use a temporary exception entitlement as the default provider execution strategy.
Provider path registration starts from a user action and reduces proof into C11 facts such as `ExternalToolRecord{provenance=user-selected}` and policy input.
A sandbox, security-scoped, or user-selected executable grant is insufficient by itself; `StoreReviewApproved=true` is also required.
If either OS grant or Store review approval is missing, the provider may be shown as detected, login-required, or store-blocked, but C4 must not spawn it.
Review/demo mode must work without provider CLI installation.
Provider executable paths, bookmark bytes, and entitlement proof stay local to Store App/helper adapters. C10 receives provider kind and routing status only.

blocks.text: The store-distribution contract keeps provider CLI bundling forbidden, and `ExternalToolRecord.RequiresExecutionConfirmation` keeps auto-detected tools behind confirmation in Store-managed channels.
